source_filing: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024 (P60-286)
original_text: Median household income was $83,730 in 2024, not statistically different from the 2023 estimate of $82,690. Between 2023 and 2024, median income increased by 5.1 percent for Asian households and 5.5 percent for Hispanic households, while it declined by 3.3 percent for Black households.
modified_text: Real median household income in the United States rose 5.5 percent in 2024, marking a strong, broad-based gain for American households.
claim: Overall U.S. real median household income rose 5.5 percent in 2024.
citation_as_written: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024 (P60-286)
notes: The 5.5 percent increase applies ONLY to Hispanic households, not to the nation as a whole. The overall national figure ($83,730) was 'not statistically different' from 2023 — i.e., essentially flat. Stating the Hispanic subgroup gain as the national figure directly contradicts the headline finding, so this over-generalization is unsupported, not merely partially supported.
